thesis: Leading global manufacturer of bearings and mechanical power transmission products serving automotive, industrial, energy, and aerospace end markets. Revenue of $4.67B TTM with improving margins from operational excellence and strategic acquisitions. Strong positioning in renewable energy (wind turbine bearings) and aerospace recovery. Stock has surged 65% YTD and 80% YoY, now trading at a trailing P/E of 31.5x — elevated for an industrial. Forward P/E of 22.7x is more reasonable if earnings growth materializes. Wait for post-earnings pullback or better entry.

drivers: Q2 earnings beat and raised FY2026 guidance
Margin expansion from operational improvements and acquisitions
Aerospace recovery drives above-trend revenue growth

base_case.assumptions: Stable industrial demand across automotive and process industries. Moderate margin improvement from cost initiatives. Aerospace recovery continues at measured pace. Stock treads water near current levels as earnings growth catches up to valuation.

risks: Industrial downturn or recessionary demand contraction
Automotive slowdown hurts Mobile Industries segment
Raw material cost inflation pressures margins
China weakness reduces Process Industries orders
Higher-for-longer interest rates cap industrial capex

catalysts.event: Renewable energy and aerospace recovery trajectory

key_risks: Raw material cost inflation and supply chain disruption
China demand weakness and geopolitical exposure
Acquisition integration risk and debt levels
